revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:People related to Japanese mythology]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24593703458|time triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[fr:Amanomichine]][[ja:天道根命]][[simple:Amanomichine]][[zh:天道根命]] [[fr:Amanomichine]][[ja:天道根命]][[simple:Amanomichine]][[zh:天道根命]] {{Infobox deity|type=Shinto|name=Amanomichine|image=File:Hinokuma Shrine.JPG|caption=Hinokuma Shrine, the traditional shrine of the family|script_name=Japanese|script=天道根命|other_names=|cult_center=|symbols=|consort=|parents=|siblings=|children=|texts=|mother=|father={{ill|Hikosachi no Mikoto|ja|彦狭知命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikosachi no Mikoto|qq=}}|founder={{ill|Amanomichine|ja|天道根命|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Amanomichine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanomichine|qq=}}<ref name="web.archive.org-2023" />|parent house=[[Kuni no miyatsuko]]|image caption=Hinokuma Shrine, the traditional shrine of the family|alt=Hinokuma Shrine, the traditional shrine of the family|province of origin=[[Kii Province]]|home province=[[Kii Province]]|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}|offspring={{ill|Q110869766|qid=Q110869766|1=Hikoma no Mikoto}}; {{ill|Q110869777|qid=Q110869777|1=Makiri no Mikoto}}|relatives={{ill|Kamimusubi|qid=Q3275901|1=Kamimusubi}}}}
{{complex|qq=}} {{ill|Amanomichine|ja|天道根命|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Amanomichine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanomichine|qq=}} (天道根命) is a Japanese deity. The {{ill|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀伊国造|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|en|draft:Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|fr|User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} is descended from him.<ref name="web.archive.org-2023">{{Cite web|date=2023-12-16|title=Kinokuninomiyatsuko {{!|qq=}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231216060822/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=8597|access-date=2023-12-16|website=web.archive.org|qq=}}</ref> They accompanied {{ill|Amateru|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amateru|qq=}} alongside [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]]<ref>{{Cite web|title=The Book of Heaven (Chapters 6) |url=https://www.hotsuma.gr.jp/aya/aya06-e.html |access-date=2023-10-16 |website=www.hotsuma.gr.jp|qq=}}</ref>. There are two sacred mirrors given by Amanomichine to Jimmu, Higata-no-Kagami and Hihoko-no-Kagami.<ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.ca/books?id=BY23AwAAQBAJ&pg=PA191&lpg=PA191&dq=%22Ame+no+michine%22&source=bl&ots=_uBc4YBzjJ&sig=ACfU3U0ixP5RxwleOWP16KTxsavnTSL8ug&hl=en&sa=X&ved=2ahUKEwi1rJvwo6CFAxVgIDQIHY4zCGU4ChDoAXoECAUQAw#v=onepage&q&f=false|title=Studies In Shinto & Shrines|last=Ponsonby-Fane|first=R. A. B.|date=2014-06-03|publisher=Routledge|isbn=978-1-136-89294-3|language=en|qq=}}</ref> The current leader of Kii, serving as the kokusoke in Kii province, proudly traces their lineage as the eightieth-generation descendant of Ame-no-michine. This deity was a companion of Ninigi, Amaterasu's grandson, during his descent from the heavens, marking a legacy that continues to this day.<ref>{{Cite book|url=https://www.amazon.com/Above-Clouds-Takie-Sugiyama-Lebra/dp/0520076028#:~:text=Book%20overview&text=This%20latest%20work%20from%20Japanese,the%20emperor%20and%20his%20family.|title=Above the Clouds: Status Culture of the Modern Japanese Nobility|last=Lebra|first=Takie Sugiyama Sugiyama|date=1995-03-27|publisher=University of California Press|isbn=978-0-520-07602-0|location=Berkeley|language=English|qq=}}</ref>{{rp|80|qq=}} In ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Shōjiroku|qq=}}'', the descendants of {{ill|Amatsuhikone|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsuhikone|qq=}}, {{ill|Ame no Hohi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-hohi|qq=}}, and {{ill|Amanomichine|ja|天道根命|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Amanomichine|8=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanomichine|qq=}}, together with the descendants of Amenohoakari are referred to as {{ill|Tenson-zoku|fr|ja|天孫族|zh|天孫民族|en:draft|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|ko|천손족|uk|Тенсон Зоку|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|qq=}} (天孫族). The ''{{ill|Tenson-zoku|fr|ja|天孫族|zh|天孫民族|en:draft|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|ko|천손족|uk|Тенсон Зоку|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|qq=}}'' descended from [[Takamagahara|Takamagahara]] (Plain of High {{ill|Heaven|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heaven|qq=}}) to {{ill|Owari Province|Owari|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari Province|qq=}} and {{ill|Tanba Province|lt=Tanba|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba Province|qq=}} provinces, and are considered to be the ancestors of {{ill|Owari clan|lt=Owari|en|fr|clan Owari|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Tsumori clan|lt=Tsumori|ja|津守氏|en|6=fr|7=Clan Tsumori|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsumori clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Amabe clan|en|Amabe clan|qid=Q65272445|lt=Amabe|1=Amabe clan}}, and {{ill|Tanba clan|lt=Tanba|ja|丹波氏|1=Tanba clan|2=ja|3=丹波氏|4=ko|5=단바씨|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Tanba clan|en|6=fr|7=Clan Tanba|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba clan|qq=}} clans.<ref>{{Cite book|url=http://worldcat.org/oclc/959773242|title=Shinsen Shōjiroku (新撰姓氏錄)|last=Hanawa|first=Hokiichi|date=1983|publisher=Onkogakkai|location=Japan|oclc=959773242|en|qq=}}</ref> However, it is clear that ''{{nihongo|'''Amabe clan genealogy'''|海部氏系図|Amabe-shi Keizu|qq=}},'' which records these four clans as descendants of Amenohoakari, is a forged document,<ref>{{Cite book |last=Hoga |first=Toshio |title=Kokuho「Amabe-shi Keizu」he no gimon, Kokigi no Heya (国宝「海部氏系図」への疑問 古樹紀之房間) |year=2006 |location=Japan|qq=}}</ref> and that these clans actually descended from the {{ill|List of water deities|lt=sea deity|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of water deities|qq=}} {{ill|Watatsumi|en|Watatsumi|qid=Q1474187|lt=Watatsumi|1=Watatsumi}}. In addition, Owari clan's genealogy includes the great-grandson of Watatsumi, {{ill|Takakuraji|en|Takakuraji|qid=Q11668757|lt=Takakuraji|1=Takakuraji}}, as their ancestor, and this is considered to be the original {{ill|Genealogy|en|Genealogy|qid=Q47307|lt=genealogy|1=Genealogy}}.<ref>{{Cite book |last=Hoga |first=Toshio |title=Tango no Amabe-shi no Shutsuji to sono ichizoku, Kokigi no Heya (丹後の海部氏の出自とその一族) |year=2006 |location=Japan|qq=}}</ref> ==jawiki content==
{{Basic information court noble | Name = Amanomichine no Mikoto | Image = | Image size = | Image description = | Period = [[Yayoi period]], [[Mythological antiquity]] | Born = Unknown | Died = Unknown | Renamed = | Other names = | Posthumous name = | Divine name = | Buddhist name = | Grave = | Court rank = [[Kinokuni no Miyatsuko|Ki no Kuni no Miyatsuko]] | Lord = [[Emperor Jimmu]] | Clan = Ancestor of the Ki clan | Parents = Father: unknown ([[Hikosachi no Mikoto]]?) | Siblings = | Spouse = | Children = [[Hikoma no Mikoto]] | Notes = }}
'''Amanomichine no Mikoto''' (あまのみちねのみこと / あめの-, [[dates unknown]]) was an ancient [[Japan|Japanese]] [[powerful clan leader]] and the first '''[[Ki no Kuni no Miyatsuko]]'''.
== Divine genealogy and descendant clans == According to the 『[[Sendai Kuji Hongi]]』, he is listed as the “next” child of [[Ame no Mikumochi no Mikoto]], a child deity of [[Kamimusubi no Mikoto]], and is regarded as the ancestor of the [[Kawase clan|Kawa (Kawa) Se no Miyatsuko]] and others (『[[Jindai Hongi]]』, 『[[Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi]]』). However, in the 『[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]』 he is described as a fifth-generation descendant of Kamimusubi no Mikoto (identical with Kamimusubi no Mikoto), and is transmitted as the ancestor of [[Shigeno no Sukune]]<ref>Lower Tenjinbu of the [[Right Capital]], [[Shinbetsu]].</ref>, [[Ōsaka no Atae]]<ref>Tenjinbu of [[Yamato Province]], Shinbetsu.</ref>, Ki no Atae<ref name="河内">Tenjinbu of [[Kawachi Province]], Shinbetsu.</ref>, [[Ōmura no Atae Tamura]]<ref name="河内"/>, Kawase no Miyatsuko<ref>Tenjinbu of [[Izumi Province]], Shinbetsu.</ref>, as well as the [[Iso clan]] (Isoshi), [[Narahara clan]], and [[Shigeno clan (Shinbetsu)|Shigeno clan]]<ref>According to 『Shisei』 (Supervised by [[Higuchi Kiyoyuki]] / Author: [[Niwa Motokazu]], [[Akita Shoten]]).</ref>. There is an issue regarding how to interpret the term “next” in “next of Ame no Mikumochi no Mikoto”: if taken to mean a younger sibling, he could be regarded as a child deity of Kamimusubi no Mikoto<ref>The 『Ki no Kuni no Miyatsuko Genealogy』 transmitted by the Ki no Kuni no Miyatsuko family also treats him as a child deity of Kamimusubi no Mikoto. For this genealogy, see the notes below.</ref>, but the tradition that he was a fifth-generation descendant is more widespread.
In the “Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko Shidai” owned by Kitoshiyuki of the Ki no Kuni no Miyatsuko lineage, he is said to have had a son, [[Hikoma no Mikoto]]<ref> https://123deta.com/document/ydvxje1y-紀伊国造次第の成立とその背景.html</ref>.
== Deeds == According to the 『Sendai Kuji Hongi』, [[Tenjin Hongi]], he was one of the 32 deities who accompanied [[Nigihayahi no Mikoto]] as a guard when he descended from [[Takamagahara]] to [[Ashihara no Nakatsukuni]]. According to the same text’s 『Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi』 and the 『Kuni no Miyatsuko Shidai』 transmitted by the Ki no Kuni no Miyatsuko family, he was appointed by [[Emperor Jimmu]] as the first [[Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko]]<ref>The original 『Kuni no Miyatsuko Shidai』 was recopied in Jōgan 16 (874) by the 36th Ki no Kuni no Miyatsuko, Ki no Hiroyo (Saeki, 『Studies on the Shinsen Shōjiroku』, Evidential Studies, vol. 4).</ref>.
An alternative tradition in the “Kuni no Miyatsuko Kafu” contained in the 『[[Kii Zoku Fudoki]]』 states that after accompanying the descent of Hinokuma Ōkami and Kunigakari Ōkami (the deities enshrined at [[Hinokuma Shrine and Kunikakasu Shrine|Hinokuma Shrine]] in Akizuki, [[Wakayama City|Wakayama City, Wakayama Prefecture]], worshipped by the Ki no Kuni no Miyatsuko), he served both deities. Later, during [[Jimmu’s Eastern Expedition]], he carried the two sacred treasures—the [[Hizōkyō and Hihoko|Hizō Mirror and Hihoko]]—which were the divine bodies of the two deities, and arrived in [[Kii Province]], [[Nagusa District]]. He enshrined them at Kotono-ura in Kemi Village (present-day Kemi, Wakayama City) and prayed for the success of the emperor’s expedition. As a reward after Jimmu’s enthronement, he was granted Kii Province and appointed [[Kuni no Miyatsuko]], and thereafter his descendants inherited the office and continued to worship at Hinokuma Shrine<ref name="続記14">Volume 14 (Hinokuma and Kunikakasu Shrines), section on the Kuni no Miyatsuko genealogy.</ref>. Another 『Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko Genealogy』 preserved by the same family records the tradition in even greater detail<ref>The 『Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko Genealogy』 is included in 『[[Zoku Gunsho Ruijū]]』, Series 7 (Genealogies), lower volume.</ref>.
According to the 『Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko Genealogy』, during [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]]’s [[Ama-no-Iwato|concealment in the Heavenly Rock Cave]], the two sacred treasures—the Hizō Mirror and Hihoko—were cast by [[Ishikori-dome no Mikoto]] and entrusted to Amanomichine no Mikoto by the deities of Takamagahara. He enshrined them there as the “preceding spirit (saki no mitama)” of Amaterasu. At the time of the [[Descent of the Heavenly Grandson]], he accompanied [[Ame no Oshihomimi no Mikoto|Ninigi no Mikoto]], who had received these two treasures along with the [[Three Sacred Treasures]] as sacred mirror and sacred [[spear]], and descended with him to [[Hyūga Province|Hyūga]]. Afterward, during Emperor Jimmu’s eastern expedition, he was again entrusted with the treasures and instructed to find a suitable place for their enshrinement. Separating from the expeditionary force, he traveled the coasts of many provinces and eventually arrived at [[Kadaura]] in Kii Province, then moved to Kimoto Village (present-day Kinomoto, Wakayama City), where he stayed for a time. He later moved to Funatsuki-ura in Kemi Village and built a [[temporary palace]] atop a towering rock rising from the sea, enshrining the treasures there and praying for Emperor Jimmu’s military success and longevity. When Emperor Jimmu later arrived at Mount Kamayama (present-day Wada, Wakayama City) during the expedition and requested an on-site inspection of the proposed enshrinement site, the emperor praised its suitability and ordered him to continue praying for the pacification of the realm. After Jimmu’s enthronement at [[Yamato Province]] [[Kashihara]], Amanomichine was rewarded for his prayers by being granted Kii Province and commanded that his descendants inherit the office of Kuni no Miyatsuko. He then returned to Kii Province, married '''Jidōme no Mikoto''', a local deity’s '''daughter''', and had a son, '''Hikoma no Mikoto'''. Thereafter, his descendants inherited the role of Kuni no Miyatsuko and continued to worship at the Hinokuma and Kunikakasu shrines. The genealogy concludes by stating that he was a “long-lived deity” who lived “from the time of Kamimusubi no Mikoto until the reign of Emperor Jimmu.”
=== Descent and eastern expedition === Regarding Amanomichine no Mikoto’s descent, traditions are divided between those that say he accompanied the descent of the Hinokuma and Kunikakasu deities and those that say he accompanied the “Descent of the Heavenly Grandson.” Within the latter, opinions further differ on whether the principal figure was Ninigi no Mikoto or Nigihayahi no Mikoto. [[Tanaka Takashi]] focuses on the last of these (the Nigihayahi accompaniment theory), noting that in 『[[Kogo Shūi]]』 the deity enshrined at Hinokuma Shrine is said to be a Hizō Mirror cast earlier than the mirror enshrined at the [[Ise Grand Shrine|Imperial Grand Shrine of Ise]] (the so-called [[Yata no Kagami]]), and that there is a tradition that the casting deity, Ishikori-dome no Mikoto, was dissatisfied with it<ref>『Kogo Shūi』, section on the emergence of the sun deity.</ref>. Considering this together, Amanomichine would have accompanied Nigihayahi no Mikoto while bearing a Hizō Mirror that was not satisfactory, suggesting that an eastern expedition led by Nigihayahi occurred prior to Emperor Jimmu, and that from the Jimmu side this was regarded as unwelcome. Tanaka therefore argues that the historical fact of Nigihayahi’s eastern expedition was later transmitted in the form of a “descent” tradition<ref>Tanaka, “The First Descent of the Heavenly Grandson and the Eastern Expedition of Nigihayahi no Mikoto.”</ref>.
On the other hand, the 『Kii Zoku Fudoki』 rejects as erroneous all traditions linking Amanomichine no Mikoto with the Descent of the Heavenly Grandson, Emperor Jimmu’s eastern expedition, and the Hinokuma Shrine. It notes that according to the 『Sendai Kuji Hongi』 [[Chigi Hongi]], the Ki no Kuni no Miyatsuko worshipped the so-called Itakiso Three Deities—[[Isotakeru no Mikoto]], [[Ōyatsuhime no Mikoto]], and [[Tsumatsuhime no Mikoto]]—and thus argues that Amanomichine himself likely worshipped these three deities in Kii, rather than the two sacred treasures<ref name="続記14"/>.
=== The Kuni no Miyatsuko office === The prevailing view holds that the Kuni no Miyatsuko system was a制度 through which the Yamato kingship reorganized local chieftains to govern regions, particularly from the reign of the 26th [[Emperor Keitai]] to the 29th [[Emperor Kinmei]] (early 6th century)<ref>[[Hisashi Kano]], “The Bemmin and Kuni no Miyatsuko Systems” (『Iwanami Lecture Series on Japanese History』, vol. 2, Ancient Period 1, [[Iwanami Shoten]], 1993); [[Gen’ichi Kamata]], “Unification of the Realm by the Great King” (『Ancient Japan 6: Struggles over Kingship』, [[Chūō Kōronsha]], 1986).</ref>. Accordingly, many scholars regard the tradition that Amanomichine was appointed Kuni no Miyatsuko in the reign of the first Emperor Jimmu, along with the other appointments recorded in the 『Sendai Kuji Hongi』 “Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi,” as unreliable. However, given that the Kuni no Miyatsuko system was likely established first in the later [[Kinai]] region and then expanded, and that many Kuni no Miyatsuko outside the Kinai are said to have been appointed between the reigns of the 13th [[Emperor Seimu]] and the 16th [[Emperor Nintoku]], some view this tradition as reflecting the relatively early establishment of the Ki no Kuni no Miyatsuko in the initial stages of the system’s development<ref>[[Kōyū Sonoda]], “The Yamato Polity and Ancient Wakayama” (『History of Wakayama City』, vol. 1, Ancient Wakayama, ch. 1, Wakayama City, 1991).</ref>.
== Notes == {{reflist}}
== References == *Kishū Domain, 『Kii Zoku Fudoki』 (reprinted by the Wakayama Prefectural Shrine Clergy Supervisory Office), Teikoku Local Administration Society Publishing Department, 1910 *[[Kurita Hiroshi]], 『Shinsen Shōjiroku Kōshō』, [[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]], 1900 (reprinted in 『Shintō Taikei: Classical Texts』 6) *[[Saeki Ariyoshi]], 『Studies on the Shinsen Shōjiroku』, Evidential Studies, vols. 3–4, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1982 (vol. 3 ISBN 4-642-02113-2; vol. 4 ISBN 4-642-02114-0) *Tanaka Takashi, “The First Descent of the Heavenly Grandson and the Eastern Expedition of Nigihayahi no Mikoto,” 『Social Issues Research』 7-1 ([[Osaka College of Social Work]]), 1957; reprinted in 『Collected Works of Tanaka Takashi 1』 ([[Kokusho Kankōkai]], 1987, ISBN 4-336-01610-0)
{{Shinto horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:あまのみちねのみこと}} [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:People of Kii Province]] [[Category:History of Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Amatsukami]]

{{translated page|ja|天道根命|version=99874834|comment=Imported full ja history}}
{{everybodywiki link|Amanomichine|Amanomichine}} [[simple:User:{{{subst:PAGENAME|qq=}}}]]

<!--all this actually mandates is a completion tier 7 ranking. We are not using the promising draft template here. If I get anywhere near implementing it, I will do so based on the actual completion tiers of this wiki rather than whether I added them when they were on simple english wikipedia or wherever--><!-- . Addition of its kanji will be strongly appreciated and necessary to make any progress on this draft. If this draft goes to g13 warning without anyone adding the kanji then allow it to be deleted since no progress will be made on it, and perhaps delink the article from [[Amenohoakari]] article. Checking the [[Kujiki]] could be helpful but it is pretty hard to find.[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 18:26, 23 October 2023 (UTC)|qq=}} --><!-- --><!--Amanomichine-->
